text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنْ رَجُلٍ قَدْ أَبَقَ مِنْهُ مَمْلُوكُهُ يَجُوزُ أَنْ يُعْتِقَهُ فِي كَفَّارَةِ الظِّهَارِ قَالَ لَا بَأْسَ بِهِ مَا لَمْ يَعْرِفْ مِنْهُ مَوْتاً
قَالَ أَبُو هَاشِمٍ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ وَ كَانَ سَأَلَنِي نَصْرُ بْنُ عَامِرٍ الْقُمِّيُّ أَنْ أَسْأَلَهُ عَنْ ذَلِكَ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Abu Hashim Al Ja’fary who said,

‘I asked Abu Al-Hassanasws about a man whose owned slave had absconded from him, is he allowed that he emancipates him regarding an expiation of Al-Zahaar?’ Heasws said: ‘There is no problem with it, for as long as death is not recognised from him’.
Abu Hashim, may Allah<sup>azwj</sup> be Pleased with him said, ‘And it was Nasr Bin Aamir Al Qummy who had asked me that I ask him<sup>asws</sup> about that’.
